I've completed my review of the fix. Let me summarize my findings.

The diff shows a clean, targeted deletion of exactly 107 lines corresponding to the two duplicate `describe` blocks identified in the finding:
1. The `describe('src/app.css — search wrapper and in-input icons', ...)` block (the "A focused restatement" companion block using `ruleDeclarations`/`CSS.match`).
2. The second `describe('src/app.css — spinner animation', ...)` block (the `ruleDeclarations`-based one).

The earlier, more comprehensive `parseRules`-based blocks (`search input wrapper`, `wrapped search input`, `in-input overlay icons`, `icon visibility toggling`, `spinner animation`) are all retained. All helper functions (`ruleDeclarations`, `stripComments`, `parseRules`, `RULES`, `rulesMatching`, `hasDecl`, `ruleWith`) remain in use by the surviving blocks — no dead code was left behind. The file ends cleanly after the "new rules placed in the toolbar section" block.

Regarding tests: before the fix there were 50 tests (1 failing), after the fix there are 41 tests (1 failing). The 9 removed tests are exactly the duplicate-block tests, and the single remaining failure (`row-menu-btn > becomes visible + interactive on row hover and when its menu is open`, asserting `.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded="true"]`) is **pre-existing and unrelated** to this dedup fix — I confirmed via `git stash` that it fails identically without this change, and it lives in a `describe` block that was not touched by the fix. It stems from other working-tree changes to the CSS in this branch.

The earlier, more comprehensive parseRules-based blocks for .search-wrapper, the wrapped input, overlay icons, :placeholder-shown toggling, and the spinner are all retained, so no CSS contract coverage was lost.
